briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in the BaaS (Banking-as-a-Service) fintech model.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling big tech's growing interest in financial data and open banking infrastructure.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis exploring how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what it means for incumbents and fintechs alike.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a new wave of unconventional credit card products, challenging traditional rewards and lending models.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-interest-rate-9.7132984?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada held its benchmark rate steady at 2.25%, warning that the ongoing Iran conflict is expected to drive global inflation higher and cloud the economic outlook.

- **[Stock markets dip for another straight week as U.S. war on Iran continues](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/canadian-stock-market-close-friday-march-20-2026-9.7136842?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian stock markets fell for another consecutive week as geopolitical uncertainty from the U.S.-Iran conflict continued to rattle investor confidence.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a policy direction that continues to shape the current Canadian fintech regulatory landscape.

- **[Xanadu set to go public as SPAC deal clears shareholder vote](https://betakit.com/xanadu-set-to-go-public-as-spac-deal-clears-shareholder-vote/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian quantum computing company Xanadu is poised to go public after its SPAC merger cleared a shareholder vote, marking a major milestone for Canada's deep tech sector.

- **[Canadian companies make their case at Nvidia's GTC conference](https://betakit.com/canadian-companies-make-their-case-at-nvidias-gtc-conference/)** — *BetaKit* — A cohort of Canadian tech companies pitched their AI and compute capabilities on the global stage at Nvidia's flagship GTC conference, underscoring Canada's growing presence in the AI ecosystem.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A thought-provoking resource cataloguing the darker consequences of fintech innovation, offering important cautionary perspective for ecosystem builders prioritizing financial inclusion and consumer protection in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Canada's population shrank last year — a first for the country, StatsCan says](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/canada-population-decline-first-time-9.7133643?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada recorded its first-ever population decline, a trend with direct implications for Atlantic Canadian fintech growth strategies, financial inclusion efforts, and the regional credit union sector serving shrinking communities.
